expected_effect: Recover calm-slice edge by letting benign queue imbalance accelerate fair-mid recentering while preserving the anti-arb posture of the apr18 raw survivors.
expected_failure_signature: If queue imbalance over-steers the fair mid, the branch cheapens too quickly after toxic continuation and reopens arb leakage.
expected_win_condition: Lift low-volatility and low-retail slices above the apr18 anchors while keeping the anti-arb improvement that made screen_0008 a raw survivor.
hidden_coupling_removed: Fair-mid recentering should not wait on the same continuation memory that arms toxic-side protection.
rationale: Use the screen_0008 and screen_0006 anchors as context, but replace the binary continuation veto with a fair-mid that recenters from persistent queue imbalance so calm release can appear sooner without reopening the broad cheap mode from the earlier state branches.
title: Queue imbalance fair mid
why_not_coefficient_retune: This swaps the state estimator from a continuation veto into a queue-imbalance fair-mid recentering rule instead of retuning an existing release threshold.
